name: shot-bounding-boxes-on-20260406-0333
idea: Shot bounding boxes on timeline: When clips on the same track are adjacent, in the same shot, and in the same order as that shot's images, render a colored bounding box around them to visually indicate they belong to the same shot. Constraints: (1) Only when clips are on the SAME track. (2) Only when clips are ADJACENT (no gaps between them). (3) Only when they appear in the SAME ORDER as the shot's images array. (4) Each shot gets a deterministic color (purple, red, green, etc.) derived from the shot ID so it's stable across re-renders. (5) Multiple shots can have bounding boxes visible simultaneously (different colors). (6) This is purely visual — no interaction changes. Key concern: this could get messy if not designed carefully. The bounding box needs to be a visual overlay that doesn't interfere with clip selection, dragging, or context menus. It should be computed efficiently (memoized) and only re-computed when timeline data or shots change.

overrides.note: User answers: Q1: Box wraps exactly the clip group — from the start of the first clip to the end of the last clip, on their track only. Not full row height, just around the actual items. Q2: Bounding boxes should DISAPPEAR during drag. Be very careful to ensure they disappear as soon as conditions are invalidated (clip moved out of order, gap introduced, etc.). Q3: Shot name label shows on HOVER over the bounding box. Multiple shot names possible if clips belong to multiple shots (similar to existing shots in context menu). Q4: Allow reasonable gaps — tiny floating-point tolerance plus a small visual tolerance for clips that are nearly adjacent.
